parsed_review.summary: CP00-688 is a descriptor-only RP22 P08 (External Integrations I) evidence/implementation/secondary-workflow bridge over 40 units (RP22.P08.M02.S15-RP22.P08.M04.S12, risk B). The pack's production_ready claim is false/pending_review across manifest, command-evidence, and construction-inspection; it makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim. Implementation (registry/service/validators), tests, contract projections, and the no-write attestation are mutually consistent with the baseline distribution (hermes_evidence:20, implementation:16, test:2, claude_review:2; micro titles 6/22/12). All runtime/write/real-data boundaries are held false; Claude is bound read-only and non-final (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false); Hermes is control-plane packet/receipt only with hermes_runtime_opened=false and no product-embedded runtime. Source contains no network/fs-write/exec; the validate script's only write is gated behind an explicit --write flag and regenerates descriptor contract JSON, not product or real data. The command-evidence matrix reports 22/22 exit 0 and the auth preflight passed; no prior review receipt or raw-output exists to reject under CP178+ hardening. Verdict PASS with two non-blocking P3 provenance/traceability notes. Command exit codes were read as harness-reported evidence and not independently re-executed under the read-only (Read/Grep/Glob) boundary.

findings.title: Command-evidence exit codes are harness-reported, not re-executed during this read-only review
findings.details: command-evidence.json records 22 commands all at exit_code 0 (node --check on the five impl/script files, closeout-pack and closeout-pack-plan validate/generate, rp22 external-integrations-i validate, model.test.js, rp22/weighted/spec/fullplan/global/goal/rp00 validators, git diff --check, npm test, and build). Under the read-only reviewer boundary (Read/Grep/Glob; no Bash) these were inspected as recorded evidence rather than independently re-executed. Independent source inspection confirms the CP688 binding, descriptor, validators, and tests that those commands exercise are internally consistent (validateExternalIntegrationsICp688Coverage and validateExternalIntegrationsICp688P08EvidenceImplementationWorkflowBridgeDescriptor enforce the same distribution and descriptor-only/no-runtime flags the tests assert).
findings.recommendation: Continue relying on the deterministic closeout harness to re-run the full validation matrix at closeout. Treat this receipt as independent source/evidence review, not as command re-execution.

findings.title: CP00-688 is represented via live_state + manifest snapshot rather than a packs[] entry in the live plan
findings.details: The live plan's generated packs[] array begins at CP00-689 (forward of the live pack); CP00-688 appears only in live_state (pack_id CP00-688, primary_subphase RP22.P08.M04.S12, next_subphase RP22.P08.M04.S13). Coverage validators therefore consume the manifest plan_binding_snapshot fallback. Values are consistent across live_state, the manifest plan_binding_snapshot, EXTERNAL_INTEGRATIONS_I_CP688_PACK_BINDING, and both contract current_pack projections (risk B, 40 units, RP22.P08.M02.S15-RP22.P08.M04.S12, next CP00-689 / RP22.P08.M04.S13). closeout-pack-plan.json and next-pack-queue.json are dirty but are control-plane regeneration outputs classified as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, not RP22 product implementation.
findings.recommendation: No action required; this is expected generator behavior (forward-generated queue vs. live pack tracked in live_state). Noted for traceability between the plan, manifest snapshot, binding, and contract projections.
